ref: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These and Esau's other descendants lived in the land of Edom.
text: ni Kɔra ni Gatam ni Amalɛk. Bɛna n-daa nyɛ Ɛlifaz bidibisi bɛn nyɛ nanima Ɛdom tiŋgbɔŋ ni. Bɛ daa nyɛla Ada yaansi.
translated: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. They were the sons of Eliphaz and became kings in the land of Edom. They were the descendants of Esau.

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Di saha ka Ribɛka daa yɛli Yisahaku, “Hiti paɣiba ŋɔ zuɣu chɛ ka n suhu bi doya. Yaakubu yi kpuɣi Hiti paɣa ka o be kaman bɛmbɔŋɔ, bebu bukaata lan yɛn nyɛ bɔ n sani?”
translated: Then Rebecca said to Isaac, "I am weary of my life because of these Hittite women. If Jacob takes a Hittite woman like these, what good will my life be to me?"

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Amaa ka a mi daa pun yɛli ma, ‘N ni niŋ a zaɣ’ viɛlli, ka chɛ ka a zuliya ti be kaman mɔɣ’ bihigu la, ka so ku tooi kali ba.’ ”
translated: But you had already said to me, ‘I will do you good, and make your descendants as numerous as the stars of the sky, and no one will be able to count them.’ ”

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Di saha ka Yawɛ daa chɛ ka chilibi buɣim yi o sani alizanda ni n-lu Sɔdom mini Gomɔra zuɣu na
translated: Then the Lord caused burning sulfur to come down from heaven onto Sodom and Gomorrah

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Yiɣisim’ doli tiŋgbɔŋ maa wɔɣilim mini di yɛliŋ nya, n ni zaŋ li ti a.”
translated: I am giving you the strength of the earth and its wisdom.

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Ka Yisahaku daa garigi yɛli Iso, “M pun zaŋ o leeg’ a dana, ka zaŋ o mabihi zaa leeg’ o daba; ka zaŋ kawana mini wain ti o. M bia, ka bɔ ka n ni tooi lan niŋ ti a mi?”
translated: Then Isaac trembled and said to Esau, "I have made him your master, and I have made all his brothers his servants; and I have given him grain and wine. My son, what then can I do for you?"

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: ni Dishɔn ni Ɛzɛr ni Dishan. Bɛmbɔŋɔ n-daa nyɛ Hɔrinim’ nanima kaman bɛ daŋ ni daa nyɛ shɛm Ɛdom tiŋgbɔŋ ni. Ɛdom nanima (
translated: And Dishon and Ezer and Dishan. These were the chiefs of the Horites, according to their chiefs in the land of Seir. The chiefs of Edom (

ref: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
text: Amaa ka Yisifu daa zaɣisi ka yɛl’ o dan’ paɣ’ maa, “Nyama, n ni be n dan’ sani ŋɔ, o baɣa lan ka shɛl’ ni yil’ ŋɔ. O zaŋ o ni mali shɛlikam niŋla n nuu ni.
translated: But Joseph refused and said to his master's wife, "Look, with me here, my master does not concern himself with anything in the house. He has entrusted everything he owns into my hands.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: N dana, daŋmi man’ ŋun nyɛ a dabili ŋɔ tooni, ka m mali biŋkɔbiri maa mini bihi maa chani biɛlabiɛla na hal ti paag’ a Ɛdom tiŋgbɔŋ ni.”
translated: My lord, remember me your servant in your presence, and let me take the cattle and the children going carefully until we reach your Edom country.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Ka o daa va bɛ nɛma zaa, ka zaŋ o dɔɣim Lɔt min’ o nɛma gba ni ninvuɣ’ shɛba ni paɣiba m-pahi labina. Mɛlikizɛdɛk niŋ alibarika niŋ Ibram ni.
translated: And he brought all these, and cut them in two, laying each half over against the other; but he did not cut the birds in two. And when birds of prey came down on the carcasses, Abram drove them away. As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell on Abram; and lo, a dread and great darkness fell upon him. Then the LORD said to Abram, "Know of a surety that your descendants will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs, and will be slaves there, and they will be oppressed for four hundred years; but I will bring judgment on the nation which they serve, and afterward they shall come out with great possessions. As for yourself, you shall go to your fathers in peace; you shall be buried in a good old age. And they shall come back here in the fourth generation; for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete." When the sun had gone down and it was dark, behold, a smoking fire pot and a flaming torch passed between these pieces. On that day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, "To your descendants I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates, the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim, the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites and the Jebusites." And Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine; he was priest of God Most High. And he blessed him and said, "Blessed be Abram by God Most High, maker of heaven and earth; and blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand!" And Abram gave him a tenth of everything.

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Di saha ka Yisahaku daa boli Yaakubu na ti niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni ka yɛl’ o, “Miri ka a kpuɣi Keenan paɣa.
translated: Then Isaac called Jacob and blessed him and told him, "Do not marry a Canaanite woman."

ref: Then the Lord took Abram outside and said, “Look at the sky and see if you can count the stars. That's how many descendants you will have.”
text: Ka Yawɛ daa zaŋ o yi samban’ ni ti yɛl’ o, “Kpuɣim’ a zuɣu lihi zuɣusaa n-kali saŋmarisi, a yi ni tooi kali ba.” Di saha ka o daa yɛl’ o, “Lala ka a zuliya ti yɛn be.”
translated: Then Yawɛ took his bow and told him, "Count the stars in the sky if you can count them." Then he told him, "That is how your descendants will be."

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Dinzuɣu pumpɔŋɔ, pom’ Naawuni kpe ni a ku yɔhim m mini m bihi ni n zuliya; amaa n ni ʒe a yim shɛm, a gba zanimi m mini tiŋgbɔn shɛl’ ni a ni kana ti beni ŋɔ yim.”
translated: Now, you have come in with the blessings of God to teach me and my children and my people; but how I will treat you, you also treat me and the town in the same way that you have come to meet us here.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Dunia ŋɔ ni na be ŋɔ, biribu saha mini chɛbu saha ni maasim saha ni tulim saha ni shɛɣu saha ni kikaa saha ni wuntaŋ’ ni mini yuŋ kul ni beni.”
translated: In this world, there is a time for birth and a time for death, a time for planting and a time for uprooting, a time for harm and a time for healing, a time for tearing down and a time for building up, a time for weeping and a time for laughing, a time for mourning and a time for dancing, a time for scattering stones and a time for gathering them, a time for embracing and a time to refrain from embracing, a time for searching and a time for giving up, a time for keeping and a time for throwing away, a time for tearing and a time for mending, a time for silence and a time for speech, a time for love and a time for hate, a time for war and a time for peace, and day and night are always there.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Di saha ka doo maa daa yɛl’ o, “A yuli ku lan booni Yaakubu, amaa a yuli ni boonila Izraɛl, dama a mini Naawuni ni ninsalinim’ vaya ka a di nasara.”
translated: Then the man said to him, "Your name will no longer be called Jacob, but your name will be Israel, for you have striven with God and with men and have prevailed."

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Ka Naawuni daa yɛli, “Neesim, neema!” Ka neesim dii neei.
translated: And God said, “Let there be light!” And there was light.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Naawuni tim’ a sakom din mal’ alibarika, ka chɛ ka a tiŋgbani vuhira. Naawuni tim’ a kawana mini wain pam.
translated: May God send you blessed rain and make your land fertile. May God grant you abundant grains and wine.

ref: including Hamor and Shechem. Then they took Dinah and left.
text: Bɛ daa ku Hamɔr min’ o bia Shɛkɛm gba, ka zaŋ Daina Shɛkɛm yiŋa n-chaŋ bɛ chandi.
translated: They also killed Hamor and his son Shekem, and took Dinah from Shekem's house and went away.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: o daa yɛlimi, “O nyɛla n tizɔpaɣa,” dama o daa zɔrila dabiɛm ni o yɛli ni o paɣa m-bala. O daa tɛhimi ni o yi yɛl’ lala, di pa shɛli ka nimaani nim’ maa ti ku o Ribɛka zuɣu, dama o daa nyɛla paɣ’ viɛlli.
translated: He said, "She is my sister," because he was afraid to say that she was his wife. He thought that if he said that, the men there would kill him because of Rebecca, for she was a beautiful woman.

ref: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
text: Amaa ka Lɔt daa balim ba pam; ka bɛ daa ŋmaligi dol’ o kpe o yiŋa; ka o daa chɛ ka bɛ mali bɔrɔbɔro din ka dabindi, ka o zaŋ li niŋ ba saani ka bɛ di.
translated: But Lot insisted strongly; so they turned aside and entered his house; and he prepared unleavened bread for them, and they ate.

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Ka bɛ daa boli nimaani yuli Babɛl, dama nimaani ka Yawɛ daa chɛ ka bɛ yɛtɔɣa woligiwoligi; nimaani ka o daa lan wurim ba kpɛhi dunia ŋɔ luɣ’ shɛlikam zaa. Shɛm daŋ niriba (
translated: And that place was called Babel, because there the Lord confused the language of all the earth; and from there the Lord scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth. This is the genealogy of the people (

ref: The king and his officials liked this plan.
text: Ka Firawuna min’ o kpambaliba nini daa tiɣ’ o ni yɛli shɛm maa.
translated: And Pharaoh and his elders' eyes were opened to what he had said.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: Ka paɣ’ maa daa ti lan dɔɣi bidibiga m-bol’ o yuli Shɛla. Chɛzib ka o daa be ka dɔɣ’ o.
translated: Then the woman gave birth to a boy and named him Shelah. He was at Kezib when she gave birth to him.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: N wum a ni suhi ma suhigu shɛli zaŋ chaŋ Ishimail gba polo maa. N ni niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni, ka chɛ ka o zuliya nab’ pam. O ni ti nyɛla nanim’ pinaayi yaba, ka n chɛ ka o zuliya ti galisi pam.
translated: I have heard your prayer concerning Ishmael. I will surely bless him and make him very fruitful. He will become the father of twelve rulers, and I will make him into a great nation.

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Ka Yisahaku daa suhi Yawɛ ti o paɣ’ maa, dama o daa nyɛla paɣiŋoo; ka Yawɛ daa deeg’ o suhigu maa, ka chɛ ka o paɣ’ Ribɛka niŋ pua.
translated: And Isaac pleaded with God for his wife, because she was barren; and God accepted his plea, and caused his wife Rebekah to conceive.
